title: salix triandra subsp. nipponica
intro: Salix triandra subsp. nipponica, also known as Japanese willow or Nippon willow, is a subspecies of the Salix triandra willow plant native to East Asia, characterized by its narrow, pointed leaves and reddish-brown bark. The medicinal properties of Salix triandra subsp. nipponica include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to lower blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular diseases. Salix triandra subsp. nipponica is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its therapeutic effects.
